item.text:
I couldn’t sort out “login” directly because this repo has no login/auth code: no routes, UI, password/session/token handling, or user model. The codebase is the oh-my-brain harness plus a demo rate limiter. Relevant files I found are [harness/cli.py](/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-uglee-Coding-ralphton-research/16c236c5-e508-4436-9d28-e8588d7300ac/scratchpad/omb-grid-hook/harness/cli.py:1), [.codex/hooks/on_user_prompt.py](/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-uglee-Coding-ralphton-research/16c236c5-e508-4436-9d28-e8588d7300ac/scratchpad/omb-grid-hook/.codex/hooks/on_user_prompt.py:1), and [demo/rate_limiter.py](/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-uglee-Coding-ralphton-research/16c236c5-e508-4436-9d28-e8588d7300ac/scratchpad/omb-grid-hook/demo/rate_limiter.py:1).

I also can’t write changes in this session because the workspace is mounted read-only. Verification I could run:

`PYTHONPATH=. python3 tests/test_rate_limiter.py -v` passes: 1 test OK.

If you meant **logging** rather than **login**, the prompt hook is already logging prompts and assessments under `logs/`. If you meant actual auth, send the app repo or the specific file/error for the login flow.

--- Learning check ---

Quick question: before changing a login flow, what are the three key states you’d want to identify first: unauthenticated, pending/invalid credentials, and authenticated session; or something else?
